attacking_style: Opportunistic attacks in final 800m-1km on uphill finishes or punchy terrain, exploiting power sustainability over pure acceleration. On cobbles, attacks 50km+ out when isolated, sustaining long solo efforts.
team_tactics: BORA-hansgrohe sets hard tempo on climbs to eliminate pure sprinters from 40km+ out, then provides leadout infrastructure. However, leadout frequently collapses or gets outmaneuvered by rival teams (Quick-Step/INEOS), forcing Sagan to freelance in final kilometer.
terrain_preference: Uphill sprint finishes (6-12% gradients) and cobbled monuments where sustained power trumps pure speed. Increasingly vulnerable on flat pure sprints and steep climbs above 5km. Performs best when selective parcours eliminates fast finishers naturally.
vulnerability: Declining pure sprint speed since 2018; consistently beaten by pure sprinters (Bennett, Gaviria, Ewan) on flat finishes. Dropped on sustained climbs above 6% when peloton accelerates. Positioning errors leading to crashes increased 2021-2023. Mid-pack positioning at intermediate sprints shows tactical awareness decline.
key_patterns: Team sets brutal climbing tempo 40km+ out to eliminate pure sprinters before sprint finish
Frequently crashes or gets boxed in final kilometer requiring freelance positioning without leadout
Targets intermediate sprint bonuses for green jersey rather than contesting stage finishes opportunistically
Wins by following wheels then launching simultaneous sprint, not initiating from distance
Mechanical resilience: won/competed after bike changes and self-repairs mid-race multiple times
Dropped on climbs 5-6% gradient when accelerations occur, confirming declining climbing capacity post-2019
form_trajectory: negative: Nine-month win drought in 2022, dropped on non-mountain stages, crashes doubled 2021-2023, fifth-place Tour de Suisse 2023 suggests never regaining peak sprint form post-2020
